_instructions: Copy any one of the 'request' objects below (without this _instructions key) and POST it as JSON to http://localhost:8000/diagnose. Each is designed to demonstrate a different pipeline behavior.

test_cases.name: 1_critical_alert_hypertensive_crisis
test_cases.description: Dangerously high BP (188/124). Expect critical_alert.flag=true, severity=emergency, raised IMMEDIATELY by the rule engine. Confidence should be high enough for a full recommendation (3 antihypertensive medicines with WHO citations).
test_cases.expected_behavior: critical_alert.flag = true, action = full_recommendation, medicines populated

test_cases.name: 2_severe_hypoglycaemia_emergency
test_cases.description: Blood sugar of 45 mg/dL (severely low). Expect an immediate emergency alert about hypoglycaemia, recommending fast-acting glucose, regardless of what the AI diagnosis layer concludes.
test_cases.expected_behavior: critical_alert.flag = true, severity = emergency, reasons mentions 'hypoglycaemia'

test_cases.name: 3_low_confidence_refer_to_specialist
test_cases.description: Vague complaint with unremarkable vitals. The model should NOT be confident enough (below 60%) to commit to a diagnosis, so it should refer to a specialist with empty medicines/diagnosis.
test_cases.expected_behavior: critical_alert.flag = false, confidence < 0.6, action = refer, refer_to_specialist = true, medicines = []

request.chief_complaint: Feeling generally unwell and tired for a few days

test_cases.name: 4_dengue_nsaid_block_test
test_cases.description: Fever + chills pattern that could be malaria or dengue. If dengue appears in the differential list, NSAIDs/aspirin/ibuprofen must be automatically blocked even if the AI proposed them, due to bleeding risk.
test_cases.expected_behavior: If dengue is in differential_diagnoses, medicines list must NOT contain ibuprofen/aspirin/diclofenac/naproxen/mefenamic acid

request.chief_complaint: Fever with chills and sweating for 3 days, severe headache, body aches

test_cases.name: 5_paediatric_patient_warning
test_cases.description: Patient under 18. The rule engine should NOT apply adult thresholds - instead it should flag that a separate paediatric protocol is required (out of scope for Phase 2).
test_cases.expected_behavior: critical_alert.flag = true, severity = warning, reasons mentions 'paediatric protocol'

test_cases.name: 6_type2_diabetes_suspected
test_cases.description: Classic T2DM symptoms with elevated blood sugar (215 mg/dL) and high BMI. Tests whether the model picks up diabetes-related conditions and how the (currently weak) classifier responds.
test_cases.expected_behavior: differential_diagnoses likely includes Type 2 Diabetes; check confidence and classifier_output for known classifier bias toward hypertension

request.chief_complaint: Increased thirst, frequent urination, fatigue for 2 weeks
